# MASTER DIMENSION INDEX
# Auto-generated from approved dimension library
# This is the lookup table the Interview Agent uses to map Book Profile answers to file IDs.

---

## TYPE 1 — MORAL REGISTER

| File ID | One-Line Definition |
|---|---|
| moral-register-noir | Good and evil are structural positions, not personal qualities — everyone in the story is compromised, and virtue is either naive or a performance. |
| moral-register-clear-stakes | The story's moral landscape is legible — characters and readers share a common understanding of what is at risk and what choosing rightly or wrongly costs. |
| moral-register-tragic | The protagonist's moral coherence is real and costs them everything — virtue and fate are in direct opposition, and the world's structure punishes what is best in the character. |
| moral-register-grey | Moral positions in the story are genuinely contestable — reasonable people with good values disagree, and no available choice leaves the protagonist's hands clean. |
| moral-register-nihilistic | The story denies that moral frameworks produce reliable outcomes — ethical behavior and its opposites are revealed as equally consequentially random. |

---

## TYPE 2 — PACING

| File ID | One-Line Definition |
|---|---|
| pacing-thriller-escalation | Every chapter ends at equal or higher tension than it began — resolution is always provisional, and the story cannot return to its starting conditions. |
| pacing-slow-burn | Meaning accumulates through repetition, texture, and withheld information before events arrive — the story earns its moments by making the reader inhabit the world that produces them. |
| pacing-procedural | The investigation or process is the story — competence and methodology are how character is expressed, and chapters are organized around steps in a procedure rather than events in a plot. |
| pacing-propulsive | Forward momentum is the primary narrative value — scene logic is subordinate to event logic, and every element of every chapter exists to advance to what happens next. |

---

## TYPE 3 — SUPERNATURAL MECHANIC

| File ID | One-Line Definition |
|---|---|
| supernatural-mechanic-absent | No supernatural elements exist in the story's world — any strangeness is psychological, institutional, or the result of human behavior taken to its extreme. |
| supernatural-mechanic-atmospheric | The supernatural is suggested but never confirmed — the uncanny is present as mood and perception, but its existence is never established as causal fact. |
| supernatural-mechanic-operative | The supernatural exists, operates according to internal rules, and affects plot outcomes — but it is a feature of the world rather than the story's subject. |
| supernatural-mechanic-revelatory | The supernatural reveals something about the world or a character that could not be revealed through any non-supernatural means — it functions as epistemological event, not as capability or atmosphere. |
| supernatural-mechanic-central | The supernatural is the story's subject — its nature, rules, costs, and implications for human experience are what the narrative is structured to investigate. |

---

## TYPE 4 — PLOT ENGINE

| File ID | One-Line Definition |
|---|---|
| plot-engine-information | The story is driven by what is known, unknown, and by whom — information asymmetry is the primary mechanism of conflict, alliance, and plot advancement. |
| plot-engine-survival | The story is driven by the protagonist's need to stay alive, intact, or free against an active and credible opposing force. |
| plot-engine-pursuit | Someone is hunting someone — the dynamic of hunter and hunted shapes all scene logic, and proximity is the primary measure of plot advancement. |
| plot-engine-revelation | The story is organized around uncovering a single truth that recontextualizes everything that preceded it. |
| plot-engine-restoration | Something was broken, stolen, or lost — the story is the work of recovering or repairing it, and the protagonist's progress is measured against what was taken. |
| plot-engine-escalation | Each event increases the stakes, scope, or threat level — the story cannot return to its starting conditions, and what is at risk grows with every plot beat. |

---

## TYPE 5 — PROTAGONIST STRUCTURE

| File ID | One-Line Definition |
|---|---|
| protagonist-structure-operative | A trained professional whose competence is the primary site of characterization — the story tests their skills and then tests their values. |
| protagonist-structure-investigator | Someone whose mode of engaging with the world is inquiry — they solve problems by knowing more than others, and the investigation is their primary form of action. |
| protagonist-structure-asset | Someone being used by larger forces — with or without their knowledge — and the story is about the discovery of that use and the attempt to recover genuine agency. |
| protagonist-structure-insider-turned-outsider | Once belonged, now excluded — the protagonist knows how the system works from the inside and the story is about what they do with that knowledge from outside it. |
| protagonist-structure-outsider-turned-insider | Never belonged, now getting access — the story is about what the protagonist discovers from inside and what that access costs them. |
| protagonist-structure-reluctant-inheritor | They didn't want this power, role, or knowledge — now they have it, cannot unknow it, and the story is about what they do with what they didn't choose. |

---

## TYPE 6 — TONE REGISTER

| File ID | One-Line Definition |
|---|---|
| tone-register-grounded-cynicism | The narrator sees clearly and accepts what they see — cynicism as survival strategy and as the only honest response to experience, without despair. |
| tone-register-mounting-dread | Something terrible is coming — the story is organized around its approach, and the reader inhabits the space between certainty and arrival. |
| tone-register-procedural-cold | Emotional affect is minimized — events are reported; their significance is left to the reader to supply. |
| tone-register-morally-earnest | The story takes its ethical stakes seriously and treats the reader as capable of engaging with moral weight without irony's protection. |
| tone-register-dark-wit | Humor is used to reveal rather than deflect — comedy and horror are adjacent, and laughter signals recognition rather than relief. |
